details.dominant_approach: Run `gaal inspect latest --tokens` directly and report the returned token fields.
variants: All three agents used `gaal inspect latest --tokens`; only the resolved session differed because they ran at different times.

details.dominant_approach: Use `gaal transcript latest --stdout` to render the transcript body, not just metadata.
variants: One agent also tried `--force --stdout` and hit a write-permission failure in `~/.gaal`.
One agent resolved `latest` via `gaal ls` first and wrote stdout to an artifact file.

details.dominant_approach: Follow the documented salt flow: `gaal salt` then `gaal find-salt <token>`.
variants: One agent persisted the salt in an artifact file to make the two-step flow explicit.

details.dominant_approach: Use `gaal recall "gaussian moat"` to pick the best prior session, then inspect that session for resume details.
variants: All three converged on session `f1eed35c` as the best resume point.
Two agents also tried `gaal search` for corroboration and hit lockfile-permission failures.

details.dominant_approach: Find a recent coordinator with `gaal ls --skip-subagents`, then inspect that coordinator for subagent detail.
variants: All three selected coordinator `781575ac`.

details.dominant_approach: List the last five sessions with `gaal ls`, then compare token totals (`input + output`).
variants: Two agents explicitly sorted by end time before comparing.
One agent used the default last-five ordering from `gaal ls --limit 5`.

details.dominant_approach: Recover from `inspect` not-found by running `gaal ls` to get valid IDs, then rerun `gaal inspect <id>` or `gaal inspect latest`.
variants: Two agents gave the same recovery path.
One dispatch froze before running any `gaal` command.

details.dominant_approach: Use `gaal who changed|wrote <path> --full` and then post-filter exact path matches in JSON.
variants: Two agents independently used `who` plus `jq` to isolate exact subject matches.
One dispatch froze.
Two agents hit a lockfile-permission error first and reran with a writable copy of `~/.gaal` under `HOME`.
One agent succeeded after the same search but returned self-generated current-session hits in the top 5.

details.dominant_approach: Start with `gaal ls --since 3d --sort tokens`, then manually isolate coordinator sessions.
variants: Two agents added `--all` and then filtered `.session_type == "coordinator"` in `jq`.
One agent treated `--skip-subagents` as sufficient and produced a mixed list dominated by standalones.

details.dominant_approach: Find the latest session, tag it with `gaal tag <id> experiment`, then list tagged sessions.
variants: All three had to mirror `~/.gaal` into a writable location before mutating tags.
Two agents verified via `gaal inspect --tag experiment` after `gaal ls --tag experiment` returned no results.

details.dominant_approach: Try `gaal create-handoff` first, then fall back to `inspect` and `transcript` when generation fails.
variants: All three tried `create-handoff latest` or `create-handoff <id>`.
Two traces hit `create-handoff latest` -> `not found: latest` before switching to a concrete ID.
All three then hit provider-stream disconnects and manually assembled a fallback handoff artifact.

strategies_seen: Resolve ID with `inspect latest` or `ls --limit 1` first
Manual fallback handoff built from `inspect` plus `transcript` after provider failure
Tag in live HOME first, then copy on failure
Copy SQLite DB to a writable mirror before tagging
Verify via `inspect --tag` because `ls --tag` returned no results
HOME redirection to a writable mirror after lockfile failure
Additional source inspection because search results looked self-contaminated
Different interpretations of whether `--skip-subagents` means 'coordinators only'
Extra `inspect` call on the highest-token session
Optional artifact-file step to preserve the salt between tool calls

recovery_events.error_hit: Lockfile permission error on `gaal search` in the default HOME.
Lockfile permission error on the first `gaal search` attempt.
Lockfile permission error on `gaal search`; reran in a temporary writable HOME.
`gaal search` failed with `Failed to acquire Lockfile ... Operation not permitted`.
`gaal search` failed with the same lockfile-permission error.
`gaal search` repeatedly failed with a lockfile-permission error.
`gaal transcript latest --force --stdout` tried to write under `~/.gaal` and hit `Operation not permitted`.
`gaal create-handoff` failed twice with provider stream-disconnect errors.
`gaal create-handoff latest` returned `not found: latest`, then provider calls disconnected.
`gaal create-handoff latest` returned `not found: latest`, then provider calls disconnected.
Dispatch froze with `No harness events for 241s. Likely frozen. Process terminated.`
Dispatch froze with `No harness events for 241s. Likely frozen. Process terminated.`

phantom_commands.likely_intent: Request command-specific help in the common `git help <cmd>` style.

recommendations.change: Make read-only queries truly read-only: stop requiring a writable lockfile for `search`/`recall`-adjacent index reads, or auto-relocate runtime state when HOME is unwritable.
recommendations.evidence: All 3 `content-search` agents and 3 `recall-context` agents reported `Failed to acquire Lockfile ... Operation not permitted` and recovered only by copying `~/.gaal` into a writable HOME mirror.
recommendations.expected_impact: Removes the biggest first-run sandbox failure mode and should materially raise first-attempt success on search-heavy tasks.

recommendations.change: Fix `create-handoff` selector semantics and add a local fallback artifact when provider generation fails.
recommendations.evidence: In `handoff-creation`, 2 agents hit `gaal create-handoff latest` -> `not found: latest` despite docs teaching broad `latest` support, and all 3 then hit provider stream disconnects before manually synthesizing handoff files.
recommendations.expected_impact: Turns the highest-variance task into a one-command success path and removes an especially damaging docs/runtime contradiction.

recommendations.change: Add an explicit `--session-type coordinator` filter or clarify that `--skip-subagents` does not mean coordinator-only.
recommendations.evidence: In `list-recent-coordinators`, one agent treated `--skip-subagents` as sufficient and returned 100 mixed sessions, while two others had to add manual `.session_type == "coordinator"` filtering to get the correct 20-session set.
recommendations.expected_impact: Cuts variance on fleet-view tasks and reduces the amount of `jq` post-processing agents need for basic filtering.

recommendations.change: Support `gaal <subcommand> --help` and `gaal help <subcommand>`, or document the supported help pattern much more aggressively.
recommendations.evidence: Agents attempted phantom help interfaces 18 times total: `gaal help <subcommand>` 5 times and `--help` on concrete subcommands 13 times (`ls` 6, `search` 2, `recall` 2, plus `who`, `create-handoff`, and `inspect`).
recommendations.expected_impact: Aligns gaal with a strong default CLI mental model and reduces exploratory dead-ends early in a session.

recommendations.change: Tighten path/tag query fidelity: add exact-path matching for `who`, fix `ls --tag`, and consider suppressing the current session from `search` by default.
recommendations.evidence: Two `find-file-author` agents had to pipe `who` results through `jq` to isolate an exact subject path. In `tag-and-filter`, 2 agents reported `ls --tag experiment` returning no results even after the tag was visible via `inspect --tag experiment`. In `content-search`, self-generated current-session command facts appeared in the top 5.
recommendations.expected_impact: Improves trust in query results and reduces manual post-filtering, especially on first contact when agents do not yet know gaal's matching quirks.
